rigidity_reasoning: Butterfly is rigid (+R) because anything that is a butterfly is necessarily a butterfly in all possible worlds where it exists. A butterfly cannot cease to be a butterfly and remain the same individual - it is an essential property of the organism. While a caterpillar can become a butterfly through metamorphosis, once something IS a butterfly, it cannot lose that property while maintaining its identity as the same entity. The transformation from caterpillar to butterfly represents a change in which property the organism instantiates, not a loss of the butterfly property by something that was a butterfly.
identity_reasoning: BUTTERFLY carries an identity condition. While a butterfly undergoes metamorphosis and changes significantly, it maintains diachronic identity - the same organism persists through these transformations. This is analogous to PERSON maintaining identity through physical changes. Being a rigid biological kind, BUTTERFLY supplies sufficient criteria to distinguish one butterfly from another and track the same butterfly over time, satisfying the requirements for +I.

The identity of a specific butterfly is determined by tracking the same biological individual through metamorphosis, which is a condition supplied at the level of this natural kind, not merely inherited from broader categories. As a rigid natural kind (+R) that carries identity (+I), Butterfly qualifies as supplying its own identity condition (+O).

Removing parts would destroy the butterfly or significantly compromise its nature as a functional whole. The unity is essential to being a butterfly - scattered butterfly parts do not constitute a butterfly.

While butterflies depend on environmental factors (air, food sources, etc.) for survival, they do not intrinsically depend on any specific other entity that is not a part or constituent of them. A butterfly exists as a complete, independent organism - it is not defined by a necessary relationship to another entity (like PARENT depends on CHILD, or STUDENT depends on SCHOOL). The butterfly's heart, organs, and body are parts or constituents of it, not external dependencies. Therefore, butterfly carries -D (independent).

Therefore, SocialEntity is -U: it does not carry a unity condition.

rigidity_reasoning: Location is a rigid property. Anything that is a location is necessarily a location in all possible worlds where it exists. own_identity_reasoning: Location does not supply its own identity condition. unity_reasoning: Location does not carry a unity condition. There is no single unifying relation that binds the parts of all locations across their existence. Some locations may have conventional or functional unity (a room, a building), while others are merely arbitrary spatial designations. Therefore, Location is -U rather than ~U, as some instances may be wholes, but there is no universal unity condition for all instances of Location.
dependence_reasoning: Location is independent (-D) as locations can exist without necessarily depending on other specific external entities. Unlike dependent properties such as Student (which requires an Educational Institution) or Parent (which requires a Child), Location does not require the necessary existence of some other non-constituent entity to instantiate the property.

rigidity_reasoning: Fruit is non-rigid because an entity can become a fruit (when it develops and ripens on a plant) and can cease to be a fruit (when it decays or is consumed). A fruit is not necessarily a fruit in all possible worlds - the same matter could exist as a flower, or as decomposed organic matter. identity_reasoning: Fruit carries an identity condition. Each individual fruit (e.g., a specific apple, orange, or banana) can be distinguished and re-identified through spatio-temporal continuity as a discrete physical object. While Fruit may inherit its IC from more general properties like Physical Object or Biological Organism, it nonetheless carries this identity condition, making it +I.
own_identity_reasoning: Fruit supplies its own identity condition as a biological entity. A fruit has a distinct identity based on its developmental origin (mature ovary of a flowering plant), its genetic constitution, and its spatiotemporal continuity as a discrete biological structure. The identity condition 'having the same developmental origin from the same flower/ovary and maintaining spatiotemporal continuity' is specific to FRUIT and is not merely inherited from a more general property. While it may share some mereological aspects with general physical objects, the botanical/biological nature of what makes a fruit identifiable and reidentifiable over time (its origin, development, and biological structure) constitutes its own identity criterion. This is analogous to how PERSON supplies its own IC distinct from general PHYSICAL OBJECT - FRUIT supplies an IC grounded in its biological nature rather than merely inheriting a physical object's IC.
unity_reasoning: A fruit is an intrinsic whole with a clear mereological structure. Each fruit instance (an apple, an orange, etc.) has integrated parts (skin, flesh, seeds, core) bound together by biological and topological unity. The parts are not arbitrary but necessarily connected through the fruit's biological structure, satisfying the condition that all parts are bound by an equivalence relation (biological/topological connection) to all other parts and nothing else.
dependence_reasoning: Fruit instances are independent entities that do not intrinsically depend on other entities for their existence. Once a fruit exists as a distinct physical object (after developing on a plant), it can exist independently - it is a complete, self-standing entity. While fruits originate from plants and may have historical/causal dependencies (they came from a plant), they do not have an ongoing existential dependence on other entities. A detached apple, orange, or berry exists as a fruit regardless of whether the parent plant still exists or any other specific entity exists. This is unlike PARENT which cannot exist without a CHILD, or STUDENT which cannot exist without an educational institution.

rigidity_reasoning: Vertebrate is a rigid property because it is an essential biological characteristic that necessarily holds for all its instances across all possible worlds. identity_reasoning: Vertebrate is a biological classification that carries an identity condition. unity_reasoning: Vertebrate carries a unity condition (+U) because all instances are intrinsic wholes with integrated parts bound by both topological and functional unity. The parts of a vertebrate (organs, limbs, tissues) are bound together through biological organization and functional interdependence - they cannot be arbitrarily separated while maintaining the vertebrate as a living entity. dependence_reasoning: Vertebrate is an independent property. The defining characteristic - having a backbone/vertebral column - is an intrinsic structural property, not one that requires external dependence on other entities. rigidity_reasoning: Caterpillar is anti-rigid because it represents a temporary phase in the life cycle of certain insects (butterflies and moths). identity_reasoning: Caterpillar carries an identity condition as it is a biological organism with clear criteria for distinguishing and re-identifying individual instances over time. own_identity_reasoning: Caterpillar does not supply its own identity condition. unity_reasoning: A caterpillar is a biological organism with a highly integrated mereological structure. dependence_reasoning: A caterpillar is an independent biological entity - it is a life stage of a lepidopteran insect that can exist as a complete, self-sustaining organism. rigidity_reasoning: Person is a rigid property because if something is a person, it is necessarily a person in all possible worlds throughout its existence. identity_reasoning: LegalAgent carries an identity condition. own_identity_reasoning: LegalAgent supplies its own identity condition as it represents entities with legal standing and responsibility. unity_reasoning: LegalAgent does not carry a common unity condition for all its instances. dependence_reasoning: LegalAgent (an entity capable of bearing legal rights and obligations) does not intrinsically depend on other entities for its existence. rigidity_reasoning: Entity is the most general category encompassing everything that exists or can exist. If something is an entity, it cannot cease to be an entity while remaining the same thing - there is no possible world where an entity exists but is not an entity. The property of being an entity is essential and necessary to all its instances across all possible worlds. An entity cannot lose the property of being an entity without ceasing to exist entirely, making it a rigid property.
identity_reasoning: Entity is the most general sortal property in ontology - it represents anything that can be individuated and identified. By the Sortal Individuation assumption ('No entity without identity'), every domain element must instantiate some property carrying an IC. Entity, being the top-level property that subsumes all individual things, must carry an identity condition (even if only the trivial 'x=y' self-identity). While Entity itself may not supply a specific IC (+O), it carries identity (+I) because all its instances can be distinguished from one another - this is what makes them entities rather than mere predicates or properties. The fundamental nature of Entity is to represent individuable things in the domain, which inherently requires the capacity for identity.
own_identity_reasoning: Entity is the most general category in ontology - everything that exists is an entity. Since it is the topmost rigid property that can carry identity, it must supply its own identity condition (namely, strict numerical identity: x=y iff x and y are the same entity). There is no more general property from which it could inherit an IC, and all more specific properties (like Person, Physical Object, etc.) inherit or specialize from this foundational IC. unity_reasoning: Entity is the most general ontological category, encompassing everything that exists or can be quantified over. Since Entity does not carry a unity condition that holds for all its instances, and its instances are not uniformly non-wholes (which would make it ~U), it is properly classified as -U.
dependence_reasoning: Entity, as the most fundamental and abstract concept in ontology, represents anything that can exist independently. Entity itself, as a maximally general category, does not require any other specific entity type to exist - instances of Entity can be self-sufficient existents like physical objects, substances, or any other independent particulars. While Entity's extension includes dependent entities (like students or parasites), the property Entity itself does not carry an intrinsic dependence requirement. An instance can satisfy being an Entity without necessarily requiring the existence of any other distinct entity outside itself.

rigidity_reasoning: Organization is a rigid property because anything that is an organization is necessarily an organization in all possible worlds where it exists. While an organization may change its structure, members, or purpose, these are changes within the organization, not changes of the organization into a different kind of thing. identity_reasoning: Organization carries an identity condition. Organizations can be distinguished and re-identified through formal identity criteria such as legal registration numbers, tax IDs, founding documents, or official names within jurisdictions. own_identity_reasoning: Organization supplies its own identity condition. unity_reasoning: Organization carries unity (+U) because its instances are intrinsic wholes with a clear unity condition based on functional and organizational integration. The parts of an organization (employees, departments, assets) are bound together by functional relationships, organizational structure, and coordinated purpose that distinguish them from arbitrary collections. An organization maintains its mereological structure through formalized roles, hierarchies, and operational procedures - employees and departments are not merely co-located but functionally integrated. This satisfies the requirement that all parts are bound by an equivalence relation (organizational membership and functional integration) throughout the organization's existence, making it an intrinsic whole rather than an arbitrary aggregate.
dependence_reasoning: Organization is independent (-D). An organization can continue to exist with complete turnover of members, and its identity as an organization is not necessarily tied to the existence of any particular external entity. The organization itself is the primary entity, not dependent on specific other entities in the way that Student depends on Educational Institution or Parent depends on Child. identity_reasoning: Agent carries an identity condition. Unlike cases like PARENT (which requires CHILD) or STUDENT (which requires SCHOOL), an agent can exist and maintain its identity without necessarily depending on any particular external entity. identity_reasoning: Apple carries an identity condition. Individual apples can be distinguished and re-identified over time through spatio-temporal continuity and physical characteristics. own_identity_reasoning: Apple supplies its own identity condition as a natural kind/biological entity. dependence_reasoning: An apple is a physical object (fruit) that can exist independently once it has come into being.
